3A_Bibliographic.citation: Nagata, J. M., Cortez, C. A., Dooley, E. E., Iyer, P., Baker, F. C., & Gabriel, K. P. (2022). Association of physical activity and screen time with body mass index among US adolescents. JAMA Network Open, 5(2), e2255466.

3B_Sample_Data.verbatim_sample_description: We analyzed cross-sectional data from year 2 (September 10, 2018, to September 29, 2020) of the Adolescent Brain Cognitive Development (ABCD) Study (4.0 release, involving participants aged 10-14 years)... leaving a total sample of 5797 adolescents.
3B_Sample_Data.notes: Cross-sectional analysis of year 2 data only.

3C_Exposure.exposure_variable: Total recreational screen time (weighted sum of weekday and weekend)
3C_Exposure.exposure_instrument: ABCD Study Screen Time Questionnaire (youth self-report)
3C_Exposure.modalities: TV shows/movies, videos, video games, texting, social media, video chat
3C_Exposure.operationalization: Continuous hours/day and categorical (0-4 low, >4-8 medium, >8 high hours/day)
3C_Exposure.verbatim_exposure_description: The total mean of recreational screen use was calculated as the weighted sum (weekday mean multiplied by 5 plus weekend mean multiplied by 2, with the sum then divided by 7)... Screen time was grouped into 3 categories: 0 hours to 4 hours per day (low; reference category), more than 4 hours to 8 hours per day (medium), and more than 8 hours per day (high).

3D_Outcome.primary_outcome: BMI percentile (continuous) and overweight/obesity (binary: BMI >= 85th percentile)
3D_Outcome.outcome_instrument: Anthropometric measurement (weight via Healthometer scale, height via tape measure); CDC BMI-for-age percentiles
3D_Outcome.verbatim_outcome_description: BMI was computed using the standard formula (weight in kilograms divided by height in meters squared). BMI was converted into sex- and age-specific percentiles in accordance with Centers for Disease Control and Prevention growth curves and definitions. Individuals were classified as having overweight or obesity if their BMI was in the 85th percentile or higher.
3D_Outcome.notes: Both continuous (BMI percentile) and binary (overweight/obesity) outcomes reported.

3E_Design_Analysis.model_type_detail: Linear regression for continuous BMI percentile; modified Poisson regression (robust SEs) for binary overweight/obesity outcome, transformed to risk ratios
3E_Design_Analysis.covariates_listed: age, sex, race and ethnicity, household income, parental educational level, parental marital status, data collection period (COVID-19)
3E_Design_Analysis.verbatim_analysis_description: Linear regression analyses were conducted to estimate associations between exposure variables (recreational screen time and/or step count categories) and BMI percentile (continuous outcome). Modified Poisson regression models transformed to risk ratios (RRs) were conducted to estimate associations between exposure variables and overweight or obesity (binary outcome)... Two-sided P < .05 was considered statistically significant.
3E_Design_Analysis.notes: Three models: screen time only (model 1), step count only (model 2), joint (model 3). Also 9-category interaction model.

3F_Effect_Sizes.outcome_scale_note: BMI percentile units per hour/day of screen time

3F_Effect_Sizes.predictor: Screen time continuous (hours/day) - Model 3 (joint with step count)
3F_Effect_Sizes.outcome_scale_note: BMI percentile units per hour/day of screen time

3F_Effect_Sizes.predictor: Screen time medium (>4-8 h/d) vs low (0-4) - Model 1
Screen time high (>8 h/d) vs low (0-4) - Model 1
Screen time medium (>4-8 h/d) vs low (0-4) - Model 1
Screen time high (>8 h/d) vs low (0-4) - Model 1
Screen time medium (>4-8 h/d) vs low (0-4) - Model 3 (joint)
Screen time high (>8 h/d) vs low (0-4) - Model 3 (joint)

3F_Effect_Sizes.outcome_scale_note: Risk ratio per hour/day of screen time

3G_Reporting_Quality.notes: The continuous RR of 1.02 per hour/day is very small but presented without discussion of clinical significance. The categorical comparisons show moderate RRs (1.24-1.32). The paper uses P < .05 threshold throughout without reporting exact p-values.

3H_Recommendations.recommendation_verbatim: The results suggest that the combination of less than 4 hours per day of screen time and greater than 12,000 steps per day of physical activity is beneficial to minimize the risk of higher BMI percentile... We suggest that step count and screen time categories for adolescents may be used to inform public health guidelines for adolescents.

3I_Rhetorical_Moves.modifiable_factor_verbatim: We suggest that step count and screen time categories for adolescents may be used to inform public health guidelines for adolescents.
3I_Rhetorical_Moves.per_unit_verbatim: Continuous Screen time, h/d 0.54 (0.37 to 0.71) [BMI percentile per hour/day]
3I_Rhetorical_Moves.discussion_characterization: The discussion emphasizes dose-response associations between screen time categories and BMI/overweight-obesity, and highlights the interaction between screen time and physical activity. It recommends specific screen time and step count thresholds for public health guidelines. The cross-sectional design is acknowledged as a limitation. The continuous per-hour effect (0.54 BMI percentile points per hour/day, RR 1.02 per hour) is quite small but is not discussed in terms of clinical significance.
